solar.title: Solar System Explorer
solar.subtitle: An interactive 3D model of our solar system — planet positions are computed for the simulated date.
solar.hint: Click a planet or its label to fly to it. Drag to orbit, scroll to zoom. In true scale the planets are tiny — space is mostly empty!
solar.credit: Textures: Solar System Scope (CC BY 4.0) · Orbital data: NASA/JPL

desc.sun: The star at the center of our solar system. It holds 99.86% of the system's mass, and its energy powers nearly all life on Earth.
desc.mercury: The smallest planet and the closest to the Sun. A cratered world with almost no atmosphere and extreme temperature swings.
desc.venus: Similar in size to Earth but wrapped in a thick CO₂ atmosphere. A runaway greenhouse effect makes it the hottest planet.
desc.earth: Our home — the only known world with liquid surface water and life. It has one natural satellite: the Moon.
desc.mars: The red planet, colored by iron-oxide dust. Home to Olympus Mons, the tallest volcano in the solar system.
desc.jupiter: The largest planet — more than twice as massive as all the others combined. Its Great Red Spot is a storm centuries old.
desc.saturn: Famous for its spectacular rings of ice and rock. With 274 known moons it leads the solar system.
desc.uranus: An ice giant tipped on its side — its axis is tilted almost 98°, so it rolls along its orbit.
desc.neptune: The most distant planet: a deep-blue ice giant with the fastest winds in the solar system — over 2,000 km/h.

missions.scaleLocked: Scale is locked to true scale during mission replay

apollo11.name: Apollo 11
apollo11.launch: Launch — the Saturn V lifts off from Kennedy Space Center, July 16, 1969.
apollo11.tli: Trans-Lunar Injection — the S-IVB stage fires again to send the spacecraft toward the Moon.
apollo11.loi: Lunar Orbit Insertion — Apollo 11 fires its engine to brake into orbit around the Moon.
apollo11.landing: Landing — Eagle touches down in the Sea of Tranquility. "The Eagle has landed."
apollo11.landingNarration: The Eagle lunar module rests on its four legs in the Sea of Tranquility, kicking up lunar dust as Armstrong and Aldrin power down the descent engine.
apollo11.firstEva: First steps — Armstrong descends the ladder and plants the first human footprint on another world. "That's one small step for [a] man, one giant leap for mankind."
apollo11.firstEvaNarration: Neil Armstrong descends the ladder and steps onto the lunar surface, with the Eagle towering behind him.
apollo11.liftoff: Lunar liftoff — the ascent stage leaves the Moon's surface to rendezvous with Columbia.
apollo11.docking: Docking — the ascent stage redocks with the command module in lunar orbit.
apollo11.tei: Trans-Earth Injection — the engine fires once more to begin the journey home.
apollo11.splashdown: Splashdown — Columbia returns to Earth in the Pacific Ocean, July 24, 1969.

marsPathfinder.name: Mars Pathfinder
marsPathfinder.cruiseStart: Cruise phase begins — Pathfinder departs Earth on its journey to Mars.
marsPathfinder.edl: Entry, Descent, and Landing — Pathfinder plunges through the Martian atmosphere, cushioned by airbags.
marsPathfinder.edlNarration: Wrapped in airbags, Pathfinder tumbles to a stop on the rock-strewn plain of Ares Vallis after bouncing more than a dozen times.
marsPathfinder.landing: Landing — Pathfinder touches down in Ares Vallis, July 4, 1997.
marsPathfinder.roverDeploy: Rover deployment — the Sojourner rover rolls onto the surface of Mars.
marsPathfinder.roverDeployNarration: The lander's petals unfold and the Sojourner rover rolls down its ramp to begin exploring the Martian surface.
